title: Method of separation of components in the corona discharge zone and an ozone-free disinfector using said method
abstract: In a method and device (10) for separating components in a corona discharge zone an air stream containing water molecules is passed between at least one ionizing electrode (17) and at least one non- ionizing electrode (12); and high voltage is applied to the electrodes to create a corona discharge zone consisting of a plasma region wherein ozone is formed and a dark region where predominantly hydrogen peroxide is formed. The air flow entering the corona discharge zone is divided into two separate air flows, a first of which passes through the corona discharge plasma region, and a second of which passes through the dark corona discharge region; and a negative pressure gradient is applied to the plasma region only so as to remove the ozone and thereby separate the ozone from the hydrogen peroxide.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method for separating components in a corona discharge zone, said method including: (a) passing an air stream containing water molecules between at least one ionizing electrode and at least one non-ionizing electrode; (b) applying high voltage to said electrodes to create a corona discharge zone consisting of a plasma region wherein ozone is formed and a dark region where predominantly hydrogen peroxide is formed; and (c) applying a negative pressure gradient to the corona discharge zone; characterized in that: (d) the air flow entering the corona discharge zone is divided into two separate air flows, a first of which passes through the corona discharge plasma region, and a second of which passes through the dark corona discharge region; and (e) the negative pressure gradient is applied to the plasma region only so as to remove the ozone and thereby separate the ozone from the hydrogen peroxide. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the velocity of the air flowing through the plasma area of the corona discharge is higher than the ion wind velocity for the preset corona discharge current. 3. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the air flow though the corona discharge area is generally parallel to the ionizing electrode axis and occurs from a tip of the ionizing electrode towards a non-ionizing part thereof. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the directions of the air flow in the first and the second stream coincide. 5. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the non-ionizing electrode is an inner wall surface of a hollow chamber and the ionizing electrode is mounted at an end of the chamber and protrudes inside the chamber. 6. The method according to claim 5, wherein the chamber is circularly cylindrical and the first air stream is coaxial with a geometrical axis of the chamber. 7. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein preventing escape of the ozone in the first air stream into an enclosed atmosphere includes filtering the ozone. 8. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein preventing escape of the ozone in the first air stream into an enclosed atmosphere includes conveying the ozone out of the enclosed atmosphere. 9. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, including disinfecting air in the enclosed atmosphere by releasing hydrogen peroxide formed in the dark area of the corona discharge into the enclosed atmosphere through the at least one second air outlet. 10. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, including purifying air in the enclosed atmosphere by releasing ions formed in the dark area of the corona discharge into the enclosed atmosphere through the at least one second air outlet. 11. A device (10) comprising: a chamber (11) containing at least one ionizing electrode (17) and at least one non-ionizing electrode (12), respective electrical contacts (22, 22') for coupling a high voltage source (20) to said electrodes so as to generate between the electrodes a corona discharge zone having a plasma area (23) wherein ozone is formed and a dark area (24) where predominantly hydrogen peroxide is formed, an air inlet (15) and at least one air outlet (16) in said chamber conveying an air flow through the corona discharge zone, and a suction device (26) for generating negative pressure gradient to at least part of the corona discharge zone; characterized in that: the at least one air outlet includes a first air outlet (25) fluidly coupled to the plasma area and a second air outlet fluidly coupled to the dark area, the suction device has an inlet fluidly coupled to the first air outlet for generating negative pressure gradient to the plasma area only, and the suction device has an outlet (28) through which ozone is discharged and prevented from escaping into an enclosed atmosphere. 12. The device according to claim, 11 wherein the air flow though the corona discharge area is generally parallel to the ionizing electrode axis and occurs from a tip of the ionizing electrode (17) towards a non-ionizing part (18) thereof. 13. The device according to claim 11 or 12, where the first air outlet (16) is proximate the ionizing electrode (17) outside the corona discharge zone. 14. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 13, wherein the first and second air outlets (16, 25) are disposed opposite to the air inlet (15). 15. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 14, wherein the chamber (11) is cylindrical and the non-ionizing electrode is an inner wall (12) of the chamber, the ionizing electrode (17) being mounted inside the chamber coaxially with an axis of the chamber. 16. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 15 being a disinfector. 17. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 15 being an ion generator. 18. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 15 being an electrostatic filter. 19. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 18, wherein the outlet (28) of the suction device (26) is fluidly coupled to a filter (29) for preventing escape of the ozone into an enclosed atmosphere. 20. The device according to any one of claims 11 to 18, wherein the outlet (28) of the suction device (26) is fluidly coupled to an exit tube or pipe for discharging the ozone external to the enclosed atmosphere.
